This is the first stable release after a long alpha cycle (nine preview builds) and lands almost everything that was cooking on master since `v0.4.4`. Highlights below, full changelog at the bottom.

## Editor and LSP

The language server integration is where most of the work happened this cycle. Athas now supports the rest of the "real IDE" feature set on top of whatever LSP your project is running:

- **Semantic Tokens** and **Code Lens** rendering for richer syntax coloring and inline actions.
- **Inlay Hints** for inline type annotations and parameter labels.
- **Signature Help** for parameter hints while you type a call.
- **Document Symbols** available directly from Quick Open via the `@` mode.
- **Find References** wired into a new bottom pane list.
- **Rename Symbol** plus the backing `references`/`rename` command plumbing on the backend.
- **LSP server crash detection with automatic restart** so a crashing language server no longer silently degrades the editor session.
- Language servers now receive **`initializationOptions` from extension manifests** and workspace root detection was hardened for formatters and linters.
- Editor binary handling, overlay rendering, and scroll-path text duplication all got fixed, and there is new perf tracing to keep regressions visible.
- Inline edit got a proper toolbar refresh, is now backed by a real registered command (`editor.inlineEdit`, default `Cmd+I`), and the Find button shares the same command binding so shortcut chips react to user rebinds without a reload.
- Tree-sitter grammars learned **Svelte**.
- Vim mode was refactored with the previously missing features.

## UI and tooltip standardization

This release consolidates the split "button + tooltip + shortcut" story behind a single golden path.

- `<Button>` now accepts `tooltip`, `shortcut`, `commandId`, and `tooltipSide` props directly. Icon-only call sites collapse from a separate `<Tooltip>` wrapper plus a bare `<button>` to a single `<Button tooltip="...">`.
- Shortcut chips bound to a `commandId` are looked up from the keymap registry reactively, so rebinding a command in Settings updates every tooltip chip in the app in place.
- The menu bar's custom regex-based OS shortcut converter is gone. Every menu item now renders its shortcut through `<Keybinding>` and the shared `keybindingToDisplay` helper, and shortcut strings across the menu bar are normalized to the lowercase VS Code form (`mod+s`, `cmd+shift+p`, `alt+f9`) so Mac and Windows display correctly from the same source.
- Pane headers use a new `<PaneIconButton>` preset that replaces the old `paneIconButtonClassName` helper.
- The command palette no longer hardcodes shortcut key arrays. Each action now carries a `commandId` and the palette renders its chip via `keymapRegistry.getKeybinding(commandId)`.
- The old `WebViewerToolbarButton` wrapper was deleted in favour of direct `<Button>` usage.
- Per batch: editor toolbar, pane headers (×10), footer, tabs + navigation, web viewer, menu bar, command palette, settings tabs, database viewers, AI chat surfaces, git panels, image/PDF viewers, and the long-tail `title=` sites.

## AI chat

- New **Anthropic provider** for direct API use alongside the existing options.
- Full **Postgres subscription management UI** with backing commands.
- **Voice input** for chat composition.
- **Managed ACP installs and sessions**, including an ACP auth recovery flow and composer dropdown polish.
- Refreshed chat history UI, session handling, and selector controls.
- **GitHub Copilot provider** with OAuth device flow.
- Pruned deprecated models from the model list.
- Chat input Send button splits into `tooltip="Send message"` + `shortcut="enter"`; Stop uses `escape`.

## Git and GitHub

- **Worktree management UI** and a refreshed Git sidebar with sections for changes, stashes, history, and worktrees.
- **GitHub issues and actions** sidebar entry points alongside the existing pull requests view.
- Pull request viewer hardened against older `gh` CLI versions and missing status check fields; files tab and empty states fixed.
- **Commit details are now shown in a dedicated card** inside the diff header, with breadcrumb tooltips.
- Uncommitted changes diff interactions overhauled.
- Git gutter updates no longer cause noticeable editor lag on Linux.
- Git remote commands (`fetch`, `push`, `pull`) no longer hang the editor UI while they run.
- `git actions` menu now clamps to the viewport so it never opens off-screen.
- File icons restored in the Git pane and action feedback is clearer.
- Git settings and reset controls added across the app.

## Remote, terminal, and CLI

- **Remote workspaces are now unified under project tabs**: connect, browse, edit, and switch remote hosts just like local folders.
- **Remote workspace CRUD + terminal support**; SSH modules were broken out of the monolithic tauri setup and hardened against the command-wiring regressions.
- Terminal learned **profiles and shell selection**, ships more reliable paste and output handling, fits its container on first open, and correctly emits **CSI `u` escape sequences** for modifier + Enter/Tab combinations.
- ANSI escape sequences are filtered from terminal tab titles, titles honour live renames, and `Cmd+W` focus routing is fixed.
- Terminal now persists alongside workspace chat sessions when switching projects.
- **`athas` CLI** gains three subcommands: `athas web <url>` to launch the embedded web viewer, `athas terminal [command...]` to open a terminal tab in the current directory, and `athas remote <connection-id> [name]` to open a saved remote workspace. The existing bare-path syntax (`athas index.html`, `athas src/main.rs:120`) still works, and `athas open <paths...>` is the explicit form. A help screen is available via `athas help`.

## Database

- **SQLite viewer overhaul** with security fixes, new features, and much better UX.
- **Postgres subscription management** commands plus UI.
- Database viewers cleaned up: schema view, data view, column filters, and the cell renderer all migrated to the new tooltip pipeline.

## Onboarding, settings, and window chrome

- New **onboarding flow** for first-run users.
- New **What's New** release notes tab surfaces in-app after an update.
- Settings search index, UI consistency, last-opened tab memory.
- Account menu dropdown toggle + sign-in and production build fixes.
- **Compact title bar polish**: project tabs now centre in the title bar on Linux and Windows, compact menu overlay renders correctly in compact mode, and the custom title bar controls were tightened across platforms.
- **New window support** with refined title bar chrome and multi-window project handling.
- **Channel-specific app icons** for stable, alpha, and dev builds.
- Refreshed **Athas light theme palette**.
- Hosted UI extension generation plus a scaffolding wizard.
- Telemetry is now **opt-in and disabled by default**.

## Platform, release, and CI

- Rust backend extracted into workspace crates; runtime and project boundaries decoupled.
- GitHub and LSP backend modules split, and the AI chat history plus ACP helpers were pulled into dedicated modules.
- Release workflow stabilised on Linux and Windows, Cargo lockfile is now included in release bumps, Windows MSI prerelease versioning is correct.
- **Windows release signing workflow** added.
- **SHA256 checksums** published alongside every release asset.
- Release notes can now be hand-written per tag via `.github/release-notes/v<version>.md`.
- Migration to **Vite Plus** (replaces the previous Vite config, also replaces Biome with `vp check`).
- CI and local check workflows unified; CI script paths restored after the `scripts/check/` reorganisation.
- Platform detection (`src/utils/platform.ts`) now falls back gracefully when evaluated outside a Tauri webview, fixing unit-test crashes that surfaced once `Tooltip` started pulling `keybinding-display` into every `<Button>` importer.
- Dependabot bumps: `tar` → `0.4.45`, `rustls-webpki` → `0.103.10`, `quinn-proto` → `0.11.14`.

## Smaller things worth mentioning

- File explorer `Escape` handling fixed.
- Project picker hover actions are stable; workspace switches no longer trigger the race condition that left the sidebar in a stale state.
- Project-tabs DOM nesting and ARIA roles cleaned up.
- `Cmd+W` focus behaviour fixed in terminal tabs; terminal tab titles honour explicit renames.
- Editor pairing and LSP tab lifecycle fixed.
- Resize handles no longer show a stray blue accent.
- Tab bar is always visible, even with zero tabs open.
- Pane fullscreen no longer kills the terminal inside it.
- Embedded web viewer gets improved shortcuts and layout.
- Auto-scroll works during drag selection in the sidebar.
- `font-mono` utility replaced with a semantic `editor-font` class; sidebar recovery is more obvious when it happens.

## Contributors

Huge thanks to everyone who contributed this cycle:

- Code: [@Finesssee](https://github.com/Finesssee) (SSH regressions, session history UI, Windows signing workflow, deep file tree routing, compact menu overlay, Linux git gutter perf fix, drag-select auto-scroll, git actions clamp, GitHub CLI auth resolution, sidebar recovery, sidebar tab toggle, pruning deprecated AI models), [@Lyapsus](https://github.com/Lyapsus) (declaring directly imported frontend dependencies), [@ChristmasSun](https://github.com/ChristmasSun) (auto-scroll during drag selection).
- Security: [@dependabot](https://github.com/apps/dependabot) kept `tar`, `rustls-webpki`, and `quinn-proto` up to date.

Everything else in this release was pushed directly to `master` as part of day-to-day work on the editor.
